published_prior_art.cite: CortezEichhornYu2022 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.14552) derives network-free unbiased TTE estimation from the low-degree rollout polynomial identity

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_saturation_skew_threshold_v1 concerns saturation-design moment programs, not rollout interpolation weights or node placement

published_prior_art.cite: CortezEichhornYu2022 fixes interpolation nodes and EichhornKhanUganderYu2024 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.07979) gives a pseudoinverse estimator for general designs, but neither optimizes this TV-envelope rollout schedule criterion

CortezRodriguezEichhornYu2024 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.05119) studies fixed polynomial-interpolation rollout schedules and high extrapolation variance, but does not prove the proposal's two-sided A_beta asymptotic
Smith1918, Elfving1952, KieferWolfowitz1959, and Studden1968 are close optimal-design ancestors, but I found no causal rollout TTE TV-envelope minimax schedule theorem subsuming this kernel
CortezEichhornYu2022 and CortezRodriguezEichhornYu2024 analyze fixed rollout schedules and clustering, not optimal nodes under the exact nested covariance Gamma_P(p)

novelty_flags.label: prop:equal-spacing-benchmark / CortezRodriguezEichhornYu2024 comparison
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal upgrades the published fixed-grid extrapolation-variance discussion into a two-sided A_beta(p^eq) asymptotic of order (beta/q)^(2 beta), which CortezRodriguezEichhornYu2024 does not claim.

soundness_flags.label: prop:equal-spacing-benchmark
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated two-sided benchmark cannot hold uniformly for A_beta: as q approaches 1 the equal grid nearly observes both endpoints and A_beta stays bounded, contradicting any beta-uniform lower bound of order (beta/q)^(2 beta).

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:tv-envelope-design / ass:round-mean-variance-envelope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The optimal-node theorem is conditional on the novel TV round-mean variance envelope rather than deriving that envelope from primitive graph/local-dependence assumptions.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The headline is a field-tier optimal schedule theorem for the sharp TV-envelope surrogate, not an unconditional exact covariance theorem.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:exact-nested-minimax / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Exact optimality for the true monotone-rollout covariance Gamma_P(p) is posed only as an open-ended question.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The accepted conditional claim excludes equality between the TV-envelope minimax program and R_exact(beta,k,q).

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace prop:equal-spacing-benchmark with the actual asymptotic of the W_beta(p^eq) l1 weights, or explicitly state it as a loose upper bound inherited from the published Delta_p^{-2 beta} variance control.
Add a short boundary corollary for q near 1 showing the no-extrapolation limit, so the optimized-grid theorem cannot accidentally claim beta^beta growth when the endpoint is nearly measured.

State the classical optimal-design comparator as a precise non-subsumption claim: identify whether A_beta is an l1/noise-robust optimal-recovery criterion rather than the usual c-optimal l2/Fisher-information criterion.
If the intended claim is low-budget q bounded away from 1 or q tending to 0, add that regime as an explicit assumption and tie rho_Ch(c) and all constants to that regime.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the field-tier rollout-node kernel remains novel, but the equal-spacing benchmark and its published comparison must be corrected before Stage 0.
